ip_outcome_note: Card name is Poipole; depicted figure matches the canonical small round-bodied purple Ultra Beast with a prominent horn — codenamed UB Adhesive by the International Police. Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (name, flavor text: 'well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world') and Bulbapedia Ultra Beast classification.

trivia_bullets.text: Poipole is codenamed UB Adhesive by the International Police in Pokemon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, making it one of the Ultra Beasts — extradimensional lifeforms that enter the Pokemon world through Ultra Wormholes. Poipole and its evolution Naganadel (codenamed UB Stinger) are the only Ultra Beasts that form an evolutionary line. [Bulbapedia: Poipole, WebSearch snippet]

Flavor text (LOT-107): 'This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.' — This distinguishes Poipole from other Ultra Beasts: it is the starter Pokemon equivalent in its home dimension, making it uniquely socialized toward bonding with trainers despite being classified as a potential threat. [PokemonTCG.io API]

In the Sun and Moon anime series, Ash catches a Poipole in the episode 'Love at First Twirl!' The Poipole eventually returns to its homeworld, later reuniting with Ash after evolving into Naganadel in the episode 'Z-Move Showdown!' — making it one of the few Pokemon Ash has caught that evolved after being released. [Bulbapedia: Ash's Naganadel, WebSearch snippet]

The Aether Foundation's primary antagonist arc in Sun and Moon involves Lusamine using Cosmog to open Ultra Wormholes and summon Ultra Beasts — the same wormhole network Poipole originates from. This makes Poipole thematically adjacent to the Aether Foundation node in this archive despite being a non-Aether Pokemon; both are products of the same interdimensional infrastructure Lusamine exploited. [Serebii: Aether Foundation]
